content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer XXX. Answer QQQ queries of the following form:


Given a positive integer PPP, count the number of positive integers YYY such that
(gcd⁡(X,Y))P=lcm(X,Y)(\gcd(X, Y))^P = \text{lcm}(X, Y)(gcd(X,Y))P=lcm(X,Y)Note that while there is no upper bound on the value of YYY, it can be proved that for any PPP, the count of such YYY is always finite.

Since the answer may be large, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two integers XXX and QQQ, as described in the statement.
The next QQQ lines describe the queries. The iii-th of them contains a single integer PiP_iPi​, the parameter for the iii-th query.




Output Format
For each query, output on a new line the number of positive integers that satisfy the given condition, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
